hardware: QB2 / bh-qbge-06 / P150x4 (2 Blackhole PCIe cards, 4 Tensix)
lever: MoE expert gate/up/down weight dtype (bf16 -> bfloat8_b). Experts are ~88.6% of weight DRAM (~11.6 GiB/chip) and a large fraction of the compute-bound denoise step.
knob: DG-local, no shared-backbone edits: models/experimental/diffusion_gemma/tt/precision_build.py::create_tt_model_dg, routed as the default create_model_fn in checkpoint.py::build_tt_model_from_checkpoint_inputs. Env: DG_EXPERTS_BFP8=1 or DG_EXPERTS_DTYPE=bfp8. With no knob it delegates to the shared create_tt_model unchanged.

decision_agreement_bar.rationale: Accuracy = the diffusion DECISION, not teacher-forcing top-1/top-5. Diffusion commits the CLEAN argmax (no temperature/top-p cushion) and #48291 already leaves the bf16/MoE/TP=4 model at ~50% argmax agreement vs HF, so there is NO fidelity headroom. A reduced-precision candidate must be near decision-NEUTRAL vs the bf16 reference. Entropy PCC is the small-probability-drift-sensitive metric — exactly the risk bfp8 poses (small-prob drift can flip an accept/renoise decision even when the argmax is unchanged).
decision_agreement_bar.must_pass: ALL three, AND generated text no less coherent than bf16.

measurement_regime.decision_agreement: Deterministic single-block trajectory: fixed seeded initial canvas (seed 0), fixed per-step renoise tokens, CLEAN ARGMAX sampling (gumbel_noise=None), early-halt disabled (entropy_stop_threshold=-1.0) so both dtypes run exactly 16 steps. The ONLY difference between the bf16 and bfp8 runs is the expert weight dtype. Full 30 layers. Metrics via tests/trajectory_pcc.compare_trajectories(ref=bf16, cand=bfp8).
measurement_regime.throughput: TRACED single-step serving denoise loop (DG_SPARSE_MOE=1 DG_SPARSE_MOE_TUNED=1 DG_DEDUP_ARGMAX=1 DG_DENOISE_TRACED=1, DG_TRACE_REGION_SIZE=10 GiB). Steady block latency = mean(blocks[1:]); block 0 pays prefill + trace capture. t/s = 256 / steady_block_s. Full 30 layers. Ranking axis = TRACED per-block latency (never eager).

commands.decision_agreement_run: python models/experimental/diffusion_gemma/doc/datatype_sweep/decision_agreement.py run --max-denoising-steps 16 --canvas-length 256 --seed 0 --label <bf16|bfp8> --output <traj.pt>   (prefix DG_EXPERTS_BFP8=1 for the bfp8 run)
commands.throughput: python models/experimental/diffusion_gemma/doc/datatype_sweep/sweep_dtype.py --steps 48,24,12   (prefix DG_EXPERTS_BFP8=1 for the bfp8 run; DG_TRACE_REGION_SIZE=10737418240)

configs.compute_fidelity: unchanged from full-model bring-up (decision path HiFi4 fp32_dest_acc; MoE matmul existing fidelity). Compute fidelity was NOT swept in this stage — only expert weight dtype.

decision_agreement_vs_bf16_reference.note: 1.0 by construction — this IS the reference.

dtype_policy.note: ONLY the expert gate/up/down proj weights change; the decision path (router/logits/entropy/argmax/accept) stays bf16/fp32.

configs.consumption_evidence: 90 bfp8 expert cache files written (gate/up/down x 30 layers, dtype BFLOAT8_B, 294 MB each); post-build DRAM dropped 13.268 -> 7.830 GiB/chip; [dg-precision] override log line emitted.
configs.sample_text_coherent: wash — bfp8 produces an equally-coherent opening sentence then degenerates into multilingual noise, same #48291 regime as bf16 (coherence is NOT destroyed by bfp8, but the decision trajectory diverges massively).
fail_reasons: committed clean-argmax agreement 0.2266 << 0.95 bar (bfp8 changes ~77% of committed tokens vs the bf16 reference).
mean per-step entropy PCC 0.631 << 0.95 bar (min 0.036 at step 3) — the small-probability drift the bar was designed to catch.
mean accept/renoise IoU 0.501 << 0.90 bar (min 0.0 — full accept-mask flips at several steps).
step-0 pure-logits argmax agreement 0.949 => ~5% of positions flip from bfp8 logit drift alone, then compound over the trajectory.

throughput_speedup_summary: bfp8 experts buy ~6-9% traced throughput (s48 +9.1%, s24 +7.9%, s12 +6.0%). Modest because the denoise step is NOT purely weight-bound: the MoE batched matmul is launch/overhead-limited (~46 GB/s effective per path_to_100tps) so halving the expert bytes only partly speeds it, and the MoE is ~35% of the step while the terminal/fixed overhead is unchanged.

hundred_tps_reachability.verdict: 100 t/s (block <= 2.56 s) requires ~4 denoise steps at BOTH bf16 and bfp8; bfp8's ~8% gain shifts the crossover from ~3.8 to ~4.1 steps — negligible. 4 steps is FAR below any quality-acceptable step count (the model needs >=16-32 steps for even the first coherent sentence). So bfp8 does NOT make 100 t/s reachable at a quality-acceptable step count.

bfp4_note: BFP4 was NOT swept. bfp8 (weaker reduction) already fails the decision-agreement gate; BFP4 experts (strictly lower precision) would fail worse. A BFP4+LoFi candidate is therefore not worth device time until #48291 fidelity headroom exists. Recorded as an explicit scoping decision, not a missing candidate.
conclusion: bfp8 experts FAIL the diffusion-decision-fidelity gate. DG keeps bf16 experts. The 17.9-18.2 t/s @48 bf16 baseline stands. The DG-local bfp8 knob is landed OFF-by-default (opt-in) for reuse if #48291 fidelity headroom is created.
